hero.headline: MUJ Online MBA in Retail Management: Syllabus, Fees and Career Outcomes
hero.sub: UGC-DEB approved Super/Dual specialization. 12 spec subjects in Sem 3-4. NAAC A+, NIRF #58.

sections.tldr: MUJ Online MBA in Retail Management covers retail marketing, store operations and management, category management in retail, consumer buying behaviour, retail supply chain management, e-retailing, visual merchandising, and mall management for professionals targeting store head, category director, or retail operations VP roles. NAAC A+, NIRF #58 at Rs 1,53,000 upfront (15% off) or Rs 1,80,000. Super/Dual spec — pair with Marketing or Supply Chain. Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (70% off).

about.body:
Retail Management is the MBA specialization for professionals who run physical and digital retail operations: store managers, category managers, visual merchandisers, e-commerce operations executives, and mall management professionals who want to move into Regional Head, Category Director, or VP Retail Operations roles. The MUJ Online MBA in Retail Management covers the full retail operations scope: retail marketing strategy, store operations and management, category management, consumer behaviour, retail supply chain, e-retailing, visual merchandising, and mall management.

MUJ's Super/Dual specialization is a strong strategic move for retail professionals. Pairing Retail Management with Marketing creates a complete brand marketing and retail execution credential for omnichannel leadership roles. Pairing with Supply Chain Management builds an end-to-end retail and fulfilment credential for category director and supply chain head roles at large format retailers and e-commerce companies. Both combinations are sought by India's modern retail chains and D2C brands.

The Coursera Plus bundle at Rs 3,999 gives Retail Management students access to retail analytics, e-commerce strategy, and consumer behaviour courses from University of Pennsylvania and University of Michigan — certifications that complement the MUJ retail curriculum at a fraction of direct enrollment cost.

Manipal University Jaipur Online is NAAC A+ accredited with NIRF #58 University ranking for 2025. EdifyEdu verifies accreditation data independently and does not receive commission from MUJ. Full programme overview at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

sem3Subjects.name: Strategic Management
sem3Subjects.description: Retail strategy, market entry for new store formats, competitive positioning, and omnichannel retail business model design.

sem3Subjects.name: Term Paper
sem3Subjects.description: Independent research paper applying retail management theory to a real-world store, category, or e-commerce challenge.

sem3Subjects.name: Retail Marketing
sem3Subjects.description: Retail brand positioning, in-store and digital customer acquisition, loyalty programme design, and promotional planning.

sem3Subjects.name: Store Operations and Management
sem3Subjects.description: Store P&L management, shrinkage control, staff scheduling, opening and closing procedures, and store performance KPI management.

sem3Subjects.name: Category Management in Retail
sem3Subjects.description: Category strategy development, planogram design, assortment planning, vendor negotiation for retail categories, and range reviews.

sem3Subjects.name: Consumer Buying Behaviour
sem3Subjects.description: Purchase decision models, consumer psychology in retail environments, shopper research methods, and behaviour-driven merchandising.

sem4Subjects.name: International Business Management
sem4Subjects.description: International retail expansion, franchise and licensing models, and managing multi-country retail operations.

sem4Subjects.name: Project
sem4Subjects.description: Major dissertation applying retail management specialization to an original applied store, category, or e-commerce study.

sem4Subjects.name: Retail Supply Chain Management
sem4Subjects.description: Retail inventory replenishment, distribution centre operations, vendor-managed inventory, and last-mile fulfilment management.

sem4Subjects.name: E-Retailing
sem4Subjects.description: E-commerce platform strategy, marketplace management, D2C channel building, digital shelf analytics, and online retail P&L.

sem4Subjects.name: Visual Merchandising
sem4Subjects.description: Store layout principles, window display design, planogram compliance, and the use of visual cues to drive conversion and basket size.

sem4Subjects.name: Mall Management
sem4Subjects.description: Mall leasing strategy, tenant mix management, footfall generation, revenue sharing models, and mall operations governance.

syllabus.note: Semester 1 and 2 subjects are shared across all MUJ Online MBA specializations. Full programme structure at /universities/manipal-university-jaipur-online/mba.

whoHires.body:
Retail Management MBA graduates from Manipal University Jaipur Online are hired across these industries and roles:

**Industries:** Large format retail, E-commerce and D2C, FMCG modern trade, Mall developers and operators, Franchise retail networks

**Entry-level roles:** Store Manager, Category Executive, Visual Merchandising Executive, E-commerce Operations Analyst

**Mid-level roles (3-5 years experience):** Regional Store Manager, Category Head, Retail Operations Manager, E-commerce Category Manager, Mall Leasing Manager

**Senior roles (7+ years):** VP Retail Operations, Category Director, Head of E-commerce, COO (retail company), Business Head (retail format)

**Top hiring organisations:** Reliance Retail, D-Mart, Tata Trent, Shoppers Stop, Lifestyle International, Nykaa Retail, Meesho, Flipkart Commerce, DLF Mall of India, Phoenix Palladium

skills.body:
The Retail Management specialization builds skills that modern retail, e-commerce, and mall management employers test at category manager and retail operations director levels:

**Technical skills:**
- Category Management: assortment planning using distribution and penetration metrics, planogram development, space productivity analysis, and promotional ROI measurement
- Store Operations: store P&L construction, shrinkage and loss prevention frameworks, conversion rate improvement levers, and staff productivity KPIs
- E-Retailing: marketplace listing optimisation, digital shelf analytics, D2C conversion rate optimisation, and e-commerce fulfilment cost management
- Retail Supply Chain: replenishment system design, vendor-managed inventory contracts, distribution centre slotting, and retail inventory accuracy management

**Applied skills:**
- Visual Merchandising: brand-consistent planogram compliance, seasonal window change management, and in-store navigation design for large format stores
- Mall Management: tenant NPS measurement, footfall analytics, anchor-specialty tenant mix design, and common area maintenance cost management

**Soft skills:** Presenting category performance and range review recommendations to vendor partners, managing multi-store teams across geographies, building the business case for store format investments, and communicating retail P&L to non-retail finance stakeholders.

comparisons.body:
Comparing MUJ Online MBA Retail Management against peers:

**vs Amity Online MBA Retail Management:** Amity is NAAC A+ and NIRF #22 at Rs 2,25,000. Amity Retail Management covers retail brand management and customer experience in depth. MUJ Retail Management includes mall management and visual merchandising as dedicated subjects — differentiators for large format retail and mall operations careers. MUJ's Rs 1,53,000 upfront fee is significantly lower. Amity NIRF #22 carries more weight at premium retail brands.

**vs NMIMS Online MBA:** NMIMS does not offer a dedicated Retail Management specialization in its online MBA. NMIMS Marketing Management covers retail as a distribution channel subject. For professionals specifically targeting retail operations, category management, or mall management careers, MUJ's dedicated 12-subject spec is far more targeted.

**vs LPU Online MBA Retail Management:** LPU is NAAC A++ with a retail management offering. LPU covers retail operations and marketing. MUJ's mall management, visual merchandising, and e-retailing subjects create a more complete modern retail credential. MUJ's Super/Dual spec flexibility (pairing Retail with Supply Chain or Marketing) and Coursera Plus are additional differentiators for omnichannel retail professionals.

fees.body:
The total program fee for the Manipal University Jaipur Online MBA in Retail Management can be paid through 2 options:

**Option 1 — Semester-wise payment**: Rs 45,000 per semester, Rs 1,80,000 total across 4 semesters
**Option 2 — Full upfront payment (discounted)**: Rs 1,53,000 one-time (Rs 1,80,000 standard less 15 percent fee concession)

A one-time registration fee of Rs 500 applies at enrolment. MUJ does not charge separate exam fees per attempt, project evaluation fees, or admission processing fees beyond this.

For working professionals, MUJ offers a 24-month zero-cost EMI plan at approximately Rs 7,500 per month on the standard fee (Rs 6,375 per month on the discounted fee) through its NBFC lending partners.

**Scholarships available** (non-stackable — pick one based on eligibility):
- **Divyaang** (persons with disabilities): 20% discount with Divyaang certificate
- **Defence personnel**: 20% discount with Service/IC number
- **Manipal University Jaipur alumni** (UG or PG): 20% discount
- **Government employees**: 10% discount with Govt ID
- **Merit scholarship**: 10% discount for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's

**Coursera Plus bundled access** (MUJ differentiator): Enrolled MUJ students get Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 (standard Rs 13,999 — a 70 percent discount). This includes 10,000+ courses from University of Virginia, University of Michigan, Johns Hopkins, University of California Irvine, and other top global universities.

EdifyEdu reconfirms fees and scholarships with Manipal University Jaipur each quarter. Please reconfirm current offers and eligibility with our counsellor before any payment. Fees listed are indicative.

certificate.body: The Manipal University Jaipur degree certificate carries the Manipal University Jaipur name, the MBA designation, and identifies the online learning mode per UGC-DEB 2020 regulations. The Retail Management specialisation is noted on the marksheet and transcript. Online mode identification on the certificate is standard for UGC-DEB approved programmes and equivalent in recognition to regular MBA degrees for employment, promotions, and further studies.

reviews.intro: Three verified reviews from MUJ Online MBA Retail Management alumni. Ratings reflect individual experience.

items.name: Anil Kapoor
items.liked: Category management and visual merchandising subjects were taught with real retail case studies — I implemented the planogram compliance framework from the course immediately
items.disliked: Omnichannel integration between physical and digital retail was not covered in depth — a significant gap for modern retail roles that require both
items.body: I manage a regional category for a large format grocery retailer. The category management, store operations P&L, and retail supply chain subjects were the most applied learning I have done outside internal company training. Paired Retail Management with Supply Chain for an end-to-end category and fulfilment credential. The Manipal brand was recognised by Reliance Retail and D-Mart during my VP application. Got promoted to Regional Category Head 8 months after completing the programme.

items.name: Priya Mehta
items.liked: E-retailing subject covered marketplace management and D2C channel strategy in adequate depth for my e-commerce operations role
items.disliked: Mall management subject felt disproportionately detailed for a 1/12th of the spec — the content was useful but could have been replaced with retail analytics
items.body: I manage e-commerce operations for a fashion D2C brand. The e-retailing, consumer behaviour, and retail marketing subjects were directly applicable. Paired Retail Management with Marketing for an omnichannel brand and retail credential. The Coursera Plus access gave me a retail analytics course from Wharton. The Manipal degree was accepted without issues for my promotion review. Career support was not relevant to my job search given I was already in the sector.

items.name: Vivek Joshi
items.liked: Store operations management content was practical and directly applicable to managing my clothing retail franchise
items.disliked: Placement support was absent for retail sector roles. Career portal had no postings from retail companies and the career services team had no retail sector connections
items.body: I operate a clothing retail franchise. The store operations, category management, and retail marketing content gave me structured frameworks for decisions I had been making intuitively. My frustration is the same as every MUJ online review — placement support is non-existent for retail roles. No connections with Reliance, Trent, or large format retailers. If you need a management degree to formalise your retail experience and are self-sufficient with job search, this is a good credential. Do not join expecting placement assistance.

faqs.question: Can I choose dual specialization with the MUJ Online MBA in Retail Management?
faqs.answer: Yes. MUJ's Super/Dual model lets you pair Retail Management with a second specialization at no extra cost. Strong combinations: Retail Management plus Marketing (for omnichannel brand and marketing credential), Retail Management plus Supply Chain Management (for category-to-shelf retail operations), or Retail Management plus Digital Marketing (for e-commerce and D2C retail growth roles). Both specializations are recorded on your marksheet.

faqs.question: Is Coursera Plus access included or charged separately?
faqs.answer: Coursera Plus is available to enrolled MUJ students at Rs 3,999 per year (standard Rs 13,999 — 70% off). For Retail Management students, Coursera Plus covers retail analytics courses from Wharton, consumer behaviour specializations from University of Pennsylvania, and e-commerce strategy courses from University of Michigan. At Rs 3,999, this is a highly cost-effective supplement to the retail curriculum.

faqs.question: What scholarships are available for the MUJ Online MBA?
faqs.answer: Five scholarship categories: 20% for Divyaang individuals, 20% for Defence personnel, 20% for MUJ alumni, 10% for Government employees, and 10% merit scholarship for 80%+ marks in 10+2 or bachelor's. Non-stackable. Plus: paying the full fee upfront gets a 15% fee concession — total reduces from Rs 1,80,000 to Rs 1,53,000.

faqs.question: What salary can Retail Management MBA graduates from MUJ expect?
faqs.answer: Category heads and regional store managers with 4-7 years of experience and this MBA earn Rs 10-20 lakh at large format retailers and e-commerce companies. VP Retail Operations and Category Directors earn Rs 18-38 lakh. Business Heads and COOs at mid-size retail companies pay Rs 28-55 lakh. Outcomes depend heavily on the specific retail segment (grocery vs fashion vs electronics), company scale, and city.

faqs.question: Is this MBA useful for franchise operators and retail entrepreneurs?
faqs.answer: Yes. The store operations, category management, and retail supply chain subjects are directly applicable to franchise and retail business owners. The store P&L management, shrinkage control, and vendor negotiation content gives structured frameworks for operational decisions that most franchise operators make intuitively. Pairing with Marketing or Supply Chain adds brand management and procurement credentials useful for expanding franchise networks.

chooseIf: You work in modern retail, e-commerce, category management, or mall operations and want a NAAC A+ MBA with dedicated retail coverage including visual merchandising, e-retailing, and mall management under Rs 1.8 lakh.
You want to pair Retail Management with Marketing or Supply Chain for an omnichannel or end-to-end category credential using MUJ's Super/Dual spec.
You value Coursera Plus at Rs 3,999 for retail analytics and consumer behaviour courses from Wharton and University of Pennsylvania that complement the retail curriculum.
consider: You are targeting senior retail leadership at premium lifestyle brands where MICA or NIFT's management programmes have stronger brand recognition for creative and fashion retail careers.
You need an internationally recognised retail credential for a global retail brand — IMRB or GCSR-accredited retail management programmes have specific international employer recognition.
You need active retail sector placement support — MUJ online career services do not maintain connections with modern retail hiring teams or large format retailers.
